plan.objective: To investigate the effects of a high-fat diet on metabolic parameters in diet-induced obesity mouse models.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment will involve two groups of mice: one group will be fed a high-fat diet (HFD) while the control group will receive a standard chow diet. Key metabolic parameters such as body weight, glucose tolerance, and serum lipid levels will be measured at baseline and after 8 weeks of dietary intervention.
controls: Mice fed a standard chow diet (LabDiet 5001) for 8 weeks.
Mice fed a high-fat diet (D12492, Research Diets) without any additional interventions.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Diet Preparation
Prepare high-fat diet by mixing 60% kcal from fat (D12492, Research Diets) with 40% kcal from carbohydrates and protein. Ensure the diet is homogenous and store at 4°C until use.
Equipment: Analytical balance, Mixing bowl, Storage containers
Reagents: D12492 high-fat diet mix
Expected outcome: Diet is prepared and stored properly for feeding.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 2 h | Animal Housing Setup
Set up individual housing for 20 male C57BL/6J mice (8 weeks old) in a controlled environment (22°C, 12 h light/dark cycle). Ensure access to water ad libitum and acclimatize for 1 week before dietary intervention.
Equipment: Mouse cages, Environmental control system
Expected outcome: Mice are housed individually and acclimatized for 1 week.
Safety: Follow institutional animal care guidelines.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Dietary Intervention
Randomly assign 10 mice to the high-fat diet group and 10 mice to the control group (standard chow diet, LabDiet 5001). Begin feeding the respective diets immediately.
Equipment: Feeding trays
Reagents: LabDiet 5001 standard chow, D12492 high-fat diet
Expected outcome: Mice are assigned to their respective dietary groups and diets are initiated.

DAY 7 | 1 h | Body Weight Measurement
Weigh each mouse using a digital scale (Ohaus Scout Pro SPX123) and record the body weight in grams. Ensure to handle mice gently to minimize stress.
Equipment: Ohaus Scout Pro SPX123 scale
Expected outcome: Body weight recorded for all mice, with expected increase in high-fat diet group.

DAY 14 | 2 h | Glucose Tolerance Test (GTT)
After fasting for 6 h, administer 1 g/kg body weight of D-glucose (Sigma-Aldrich, G8270) via intraperitoneal injection. Measure blood glucose levels at 0, 30, 60, and 120 min using a glucometer (Accu-Chek Aviva).
Equipment: Accu-Chek Aviva glucometer, Syringes for injection
Reagents: D-glucose solution (1 g/kg)
Expected outcome: Blood glucose levels measured at specified time points, with expected higher levels in high-fat diet group.

DAY 28 | 1.5 h | Serum Lipid Profile
At the end of the 8-week intervention, collect blood via cardiac puncture under anesthesia (isoflurane). Centrifuge at 3,000 × g for 10 min at 4°C to separate serum. Store serum at -80°C until analysis.
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5424), Isoflurane anesthesia setup
Expected outcome: Serum collected and stored for lipid analysis.

DAY 29 | 2 h | Serum Lipid Analysis
Analyze serum samples for total cholesterol and triglycerides using an enzymatic assay kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 98174) following the manufacturer's protocol. Measure absorbance at 500 nm using a microplate reader (BioTek Synergy HTX).
Equipment: BioTek Synergy HTX microplate reader
Reagents: Total cholesterol assay kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific), Triglyceride assay kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Expected outcome: Total cholesterol and triglyceride levels quantified, with expected higher levels in high-fat diet group.

DAY 30 | 3 h | Data Analysis
Compile data from body weight, GTT, and serum lipid profiles. Use GraphPad Prism 9 for statistical analysis. Perform t-tests to compare means between high-fat diet and control groups, with significance set at p<0.05.
Equipment: GraphPad Prism 9
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined between dietary groups for all measured parameters.

assumptions: Mice will respond predictably to dietary interventions based on previous studies.
The high-fat diet will induce obesity and metabolic changes in the mice.
Measurement techniques will yield reliable and reproducible results.
decision_criteria: Proceed if body weight increase in high-fat diet group is ≥10% compared to control.
Proceed if blood glucose levels at 120 min post-GTT are significantly higher (p<0.05) in high-fat diet group.
Proceed if serum total cholesterol levels exceed 200 mg/dL in high-fat diet group.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of high-fat diet for all mice.
Standardize the time of day for all measurements to minimize variability.
Calibrate the glucometer before each use to ensure accuracy.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: >=10 percentage points (pilot fallback) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To investigate the effects of a high-fat diet on metabolic parameters in diet-induced obesity mouse models.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
